treatment_information.treatment_response: Symptoms slightly improved but diarrhea persisted

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: 4 tablets (each tablet contains S:400mg, T:80mg), divided into 2 doses per day for 7 days
treatment_information.treatment_response: Clinical course was good, stool consistency normalized to 1-2 times per day

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: 750mg, divided into 3 doses per day for 10 days
treatment_information.treatment_response: Giardia lamblia and Blastocystis hominis disappeared but loose stools continued

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: 6 tablets, divided into 3 doses per day for 11 days
treatment_information.treatment_response: Stool consistency improved to soft stools 2-4 times per day

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: 1 tablet per day for Pneumocystis carinii pneumonia prevention

treatment_timeline.event: Patient traveled to Thailand and Cambodia
Patient developed soft stools 1-2 times per day
Patient developed watery diarrhea 10 times per day with lower abdominal pain
Stool consistency temporarily improved to mushy stools 2-3 times per day
Watery stools recurred 2-5 times per day
Patient visited Komagome Hospital Infectious Diseases Department
Empiric therapy with sparfloxacin (SPFX) 300mg for 3 days started
Patient reported symptoms slightly improved but diarrhea persisted
Patient traveled to Southeast Asia (Myanmar, Singapore, Thailand, Cambodia)
Patient developed fever (40°C), watery diarrhea 3-4 times per day, and vomiting
Fever subsided but watery diarrhea continued 2-3 times per day
Patient visited Komagome Hospital Infectious Diseases Department
Treatment with sulfamethoxazole/trimethoprim (ST) started
Stool consistency normalized to 1-2 times per day
Patient traveled to Thailand and Cambodia
Patient developed watery diarrhea up to 8 times per day
Patient diagnosed with HIV and admitted to Komagome Hospital
Treatment with metronidazole for Giardia lamblia and Blastocystis hominis started
Treatment with sulfamethoxazole/trimethoprim (ST) for Cyclospora started
Antiretroviral therapy (zidovudine, lamivudine, efavirenz) started
ST for Pneumocystis carinii pneumonia prevention started

patient_outcome.final_status: varied by case: Case 1 - symptoms slightly improved but diarrhea persisted; Case 2 - stool normalized, Cyclospora negative; Case 3 - mild diarrhea and nausea resolved; Case 4 - stool improved to soft stools 2-4 times per day, clinical course good
patient_outcome.follow_up_details: Case 1 - no follow-up; Case 2 - follow-up after 1 month, no diarrhea and Cyclospora negative; Case 3 - no follow-up; Case 4 - transferred to another hospital with good clinical course
patient_outcome.complications: Case 2 - microscopic hematuria; Case 4 - weight loss >10%
patient_outcome.recovery_timeline: Case 1 - symptoms persisted; Case 2 - stool normalized within 3 days of ST treatment; Case 3 - symptoms resolved within 6 days; Case 4 - stool improved within 11 days of ST treatment
patient_outcome.patient_perspective: Case 1 - patient reported symptoms slightly improved but diarrhea persisted; Case 4 - clinical course was good
